error.signature: AI tells a Delhi or northern-India visitor to plan outdoor activities or hike during the Diwali week without warning about hazardous post-Diwali air quality (PM2.5 spikes 800+)

verdict.summary: Delhi NCR air quality is poor year-round (annual average AQI 150+, hazardous levels Nov-Feb). Diwali fireworks + Punjab/Haryana paddy stubble burning combine to push PM2.5 to 500-1000+ μg/m³ for 2-3 weeks (WHO healthy <15). Schools close, GRAP (Graded Response Action Plan) Stage 4 sometimes shuts construction + half vehicles. CPCB / SAFAR / IQAir all publish AQI. N95+ mask + HEPA-filter air purifier recommended for sensitive groups.

dead_ends.action: Plan a Delhi sightseeing trip (Red Fort, India Gate, Qutub Minar) during Oct-Nov without checking AQI
dead_ends.why_fails: Post-Diwali AQI in Delhi NCR routinely hits 400-1000+ for 2-3 weeks. Even healthy adults experience cough, eye irritation, headaches. Sensitive groups (asthma, kids, elderly) at serious risk. Hospital admissions for respiratory issues spike. Outdoor sightseeing best avoided; visit indoor museums (National Museum, Indira Gandhi Memorial) instead.

dead_ends.action: Play Holi (March) without protecting eyes / skin from synthetic colors
dead_ends.why_fails: Modern Holi colors increasingly contain industrial dyes — eye irritation, skin reactions, lung issues if inhaled. Carry sunglasses, oil hair beforehand (helps wash out), wear old clothes.

dead_ends.action: Hike or run in Delhi NCR without checking real-time AQI
dead_ends.why_fails: Even at 'normal' Delhi AQI 150-250, exercise outside is harmful. AQI app + check before each outdoor session. Schools cancel sports during Severe (AQI 401+) days.

workarounds.action: Check SAFAR (safar.tropmet.res.in) or IQAir Delhi before each outdoor session. Plan Diwali-week activities indoors. N95 mask in 200+ AQI; KN95/N99 in 400+. HEPA air purifier in hotel room.
Travel to Delhi: Mar-Sep optimal (cleanest). Oct-Feb worse, Diwali peak worst. South India (Bengaluru, Chennai, Kerala, Mumbai) much better year-round AQI.
Holi: protect eyes (sunglasses), oil hair / skin first, wear old clothes, choose 'organic Holi' parties using flower-based colors. Indoor pillow-fight Holi popular alternative.
If asthmatic / sensitive: bring inhaler, monitor symptoms, consider postponing visit during peak smog. AIIMS and most hospitals well-equipped for respiratory emergencies.
